questions.question: Describe the fundamental difference between a P problem and an NP problem. Provide an example of each.
questions.hint: Focus on the implications of membership in each class.

questions.question: Explain why the Cook-Levin theorem is significant. What does it imply for the relationship between NP and NP-complete problems?
questions.hint: Refer to the relationship between NP and NP-complete problems.

questions.question: A problem is NP-complete if every problem in NP can be reduced to it in polynomial time. What is the implication of this definition on the computational complexity of NP-complete problems?
questions.hint: Focus on the computational resources required to solve NP-complete problems.
